```

All three research streams are back, and the picture is clear. Let me synthesize before asking a couple of scoping questions.

What's solid and clearly in-scope (works reliably): ChordPro format knowledge + output templates, the chordpro CLI (render/transpose/validate), and crucially a2crd — the official "chords-over-lyrics → ChordPro" converter that ships with the CLI. Plus lyrics-source knowledge (LRCLIB gives free time-synced .lrc; Mojim/KKBOX/Musixmatch/NetEase each with their access/legal caveats) and an interactive "fill in the gaps" flow.

What's genuinely limited (the research was blunt about this): audio/link → chords is an ~80%-accurate draft assistant, not a one-shot oracle. Best open-source path is yt-dlp (supports YouTube/Bilibili/SoundCloud) → chord-extractor/Chordino → assemble, but chord accuracy caps ~80% on simple pop and macOS has a painful Vamp-plugin install dance. It needs a "VERIFY THIS" header and human correction, and there are real ToS/copyright caveats.

So the skill should be a router that degrades gracefully: render/transpose an existing .cho → convert pasted chords-over-lyrics via a2crd → fetch lyrics + interactively fill chords → best-effort audio pipeline (honest about limits). A few decisions genuinely change the shape:
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Planning: /Users/david/Documents/Program/agent-skills/.claude/plans/https-www-chordpro-org-https-github-com-fluffy-ocean.md
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
←  ☐ Audio pipeline  ☐ Skill name  ☐ Catalog group  ✔ Submit  →

How far should the audio/link → chords pipeline go? (Research verdict: it's an ~80%-accurate draft, and turnkey chord-extraction is fragile on macOS due to Vamp-plugin install pain.)

❯ 1. Guided + documented (Rec.)
     Ship the honest pipeline as a reference with copy-paste yt-dlp/ACR commands; the agent guides you step-by-step and installs tools on demand. No fragile turnkey script that breaks on macOS. Reliable, still gets you there.
  2. Best-effort turnkey script
     Also ship audio-to-chords.py (PEP 723: yt-dlp + chord-extractor) that runs end-to-end where deps allow, degrading with clear guidance. Closer to 'paste a link and go', but fragile across platforms.
  3. Skip audio for v1
     Focus on format + a2crd + lyrics + interactive chord-filling. Defer the audio pipeline to a TODO P? item to add later.
  4. Type something.
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  5. Chat about this
```

1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"strings"
6	
7		tea "charm.land/bubbletea/v2"
8		lg "charm.land/lipgloss/v2"
9	
10		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/engine"
11		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/lang"
12	)
13	
14	// View renders the two-box layout (input, result) plus a status/help footer.
15	// AltScreen + mouse are set declaratively (the v2 way).
16	func (m Model) View() tea.View {
17		if !m.ready {
18			return altView("initializing…")
19		}
20		if m.overlay != overlayNone {
21			return altView(lg.JoinVertical(lg.Left, m.overlayBody(), m.overlayFooter()))
22		}
23	
24		inStyle := m.st.input
25		if m.ta.Focused() {
26			inStyle = m.st.inputHi
27		}
28		input := inStyle.Width(m.width - 2).Render(m.ta.View())
29	
30		// The result box is kept at a fixed height (viewport height) so the layout
31		// never jumps between the "translating…" placeholder and streamed output.
32		resStyle := m.st.result
33		if m.focus == focusOutput {
34			resStyle = m.st.resultHi
35		}
36		result := resStyle.Width(m.width - 2).Height(m.resultH).Render(m.vp.View())
37		footer := m.st.footer.Width(m.width).Render(m.statusLine())
38	
39		return altView(lg.JoinVertical(lg.Left, input, result, footer))
40	}
41	
42	// overlayBody renders the active picker's list (or its loading/empty state).
43	func (m Model) overlayBody() string {
44		switch m.overlay {
45		case overlayHistory:
46			return m.hist.View()
47		case overlayLang:
48			return m.langList.View()
49		case overlayModel:
50			switch {
51			case m.modelsLoading:
52				return m.sp.View() + " " + m.st.dim.Render("loading models…")
53			case m.modelsErr != nil:
54				return m.st.warn.Render("⚠ " + m.modelsErr.Error())
55			case len(m.cachedModels) == 0:
56				return m.st.warn.Render("no models available (is the provider up?)")
57			default:
58				return m.modelList.View()
59			}
60		case overlaySuggest:
61			return m.suggestList.View()
62		case overlayPreset:
63			return m.presetList.View()
64		}
65		return ""
66	}
67	
68	func (m Model) overlayFooter() string {
69		hint := "↵ select  esc close  ^c quit"
70		switch m.overlay {
71		case overlayHistory:
72			hint = "↵ recall  esc close  ^c quit"
73		case overlaySuggest:
74			hint = "↵ look up  esc close  ^c quit"
75		}
76		return m.st.footer.Width(m.width).Render(m.st.dim.Render(hint))
77	}
78	
79	// altView wraps content in a full-screen (alt-screen) tea.View with mouse on.
80	func altView(content string) tea.View {
81		v := tea.NewView(content)
82		v.AltScreen = true
83		v.MouseMode = tea.MouseModeCellMotion
84		return v
85	}
86	
87	// statusLine renders the footer with the current transient state.
88	func (m Model) statusLine() string { return m.footerContent(false) }
89	
90	// footerHeight measures how many rows the footer occupies at the current width.
91	// It reserves the widest transient state ("translating") so the layout never
92	// jumps when that segment appears mid-flight.
93	func (m Model) footerHeight() int {
94		if m.width <= 0 {
95			return 1
96		}
97		return lg.Height(m.st.footer.Width(m.width).Render(m.footerContent(true)))
98	}
99	
100	// footerContent builds the footer string. forceState reserves the transient
101	// "translating" segment (for height measurement); otherwise the actual state is
102	// shown. lipgloss word-wraps it to the width, so narrow terminals get 2+ rows.
103	func (m Model) footerContent(forceState bool) string {
104		live := m.st.liveOff.Render("live○")
105		if m.live {
106			live = m.st.liveOn.Render("live●")
107		}
108	
109		var state string
110		if forceState {
111			state = m.st.dim.Render("⠿ translating")
112		} else if m.flash != "" {
113			state = m.st.liveOn.Render(m.flash)
114		} else {
115			switch m.status {
116			case statusTranslating:
117				state = m.sp.View() + " " + m.st.dim.Render("translating")
118			case statusError:
119				state = m.st.errText.Render("error")
120			}
121		}
122	
123		// Learn mode: bidirectional tutor. No engine cycle or style (it has its own
124		// prompt); shows the direction pair + the LLM that served it.
125		if m.learn {
126			left := m.st.label.Render(fmt.Sprintf("learn %s⇄%s", m.target, m.pairWith))
127			engineSeg := m.curEngine
128			if m.curModel != "" {
129				if engineSeg != "" {
130					engineSeg = fmt.Sprintf("%s (%s)", engineSeg, m.curModel)
131				} else {
132					engineSeg = m.curModel
133				}
134			}
135			if engineSeg != "" {
136				left += m.st.dim.Render(" · ") + m.st.dim.Render(engineSeg)
137			}
138			left += m.st.dim.Render(" · ") + live
139			if state != "" {
140				left += "  " + state
141			}
142			return left + "  " + m.st.dim.Render("↵ learn  ^y copy  ^s speak  ^l live  ^n exit  ^t lang  ^p model  ^u clear  ⇥ focus  ^r history  ^c quit")
143		}
144	
145		// Dictionary mode: no source→target pair (English-only lookups per script).
146		if m.active().Mode == engine.ModeDict {
147			left := strings.Join([]string{
148				m.st.label.Render("dictionary (zh↔en)"),
149				live,
150			}, m.st.dim.Render(" · "))
151			if state != "" {
152				left += "  " + state
153			}
154			return left + "  " + m.st.dim.Render("↵ define  ^y copy  ^s speak  ^l live  ^e engine  ^u clear  ⇥ focus  ^r history  ^c quit")
155		}
156	
157		pair := fmt.Sprintf("%s→%s", lang.Name(m.source), lang.Name(m.target))
158		if m.pair && m.pairWith != "" {
159			pair = fmt.Sprintf("pair %s⇄%s", m.target, m.pairWith) // compact codes
160		}
161	
162		// Engine segment: the selected engine name, plus the model that actually
163		// served the last result (once known).
164		engineSeg := m.active().Name
165		if m.curEngine != "" && m.curEngine != m.active().Name {
166			engineSeg = m.curEngine // chain fell back to a specific engine
167		}
168		if m.curModel != "" {
169			engineSeg = fmt.Sprintf("%s (%s)", engineSeg, m.curModel)
170		}
171	
172		left := strings.Join([]string{
173			m.st.label.Render(pair),
174			m.st.dim.Render(engineSeg),
175			m.st.dim.Render("style:" + m.preset),
176			live,
177		}, m.st.dim.Render(" · "))
178		if state != "" {
179			left += "  " + state
180		}
181	
182		help := m.st.dim.Render("↵ translate  ^y copy  ^s speak  ^l live  ^e engine  ^t lang  ^p model  ^o style  ^g pair  ^u clear  ⇥ focus  ^r history  ^c quit")
183		return left + "  " + help
184	}
185	
186	// renderResult formats a completed translation, a dictionary entry, or a ranked
187	// "did you mean" list when a dictionary lookup missed.
188	func (m Model) renderResult(res engine.TranslateResult) string {
189		if res.Learn != nil {
190			return m.renderLearn(res)
191		}
192		if res.Dictionary != nil {
193			return m.renderDictionary(res)
194		}
195		if len(res.Suggestions) > 0 {
196			return m.renderSuggestions(res)
197		}
198		var b strings.Builder
199		b.WriteString(m.st.trans.Render(res.Translation))
200	
201		if res.DetectedSource != "" && (m.source == "" || m.source == "auto") {
202			b.WriteString("\n" + m.st.dim.Render("detected: "+lang.Name(res.DetectedSource)))
203		}
204		for _, a := range res.Alternatives {
205			b.WriteString("\n" + m.st.alt.Render("~ "+a))
206		}
207		if res.Notes != "" {
208			b.WriteString("\n" + m.st.notes.Render("ⓘ "+res.Notes))
209		}
210		for _, w := range res.Warnings {
211			b.WriteString("\n" + m.st.warn.Render("⚠ "+w))
212		}
213		// A truncation warning already tells the user to press Enter to retry; the
214		// engine-switch hint only fits chain-fallback warnings, so skip it there.
215		if len(res.Warnings) > 0 && !res.Truncated {
216			b.WriteString("\n" + m.st.dim.Render("(^e switch engine · check the model/provider)"))
217		}
218		return b.String()
219	}
220	
221	// renderDictionary formats a dictionary lookup for the result pane.
222	func (m Model) renderDictionary(res engine.TranslateResult) string {
223		d := res.Dictionary
224		var b strings.Builder
225		head := d.Word
226		if d.Phonetic != "" {
227			head += "  " + d.Phonetic
228		}
229		b.WriteString(m.st.trans.Render(head))
230		for _, mn := range d.Meanings {
231			b.WriteString("\n" + m.st.notes.Render(mn.PartOfSpeech))
232			for i, def := range mn.Definitions {
233				if i >= 3 {
234					break
235				}
236				b.WriteString("\n" + m.st.alt.Render("• "+def.Text))
237			}
238		}
239		return b.String()
240	}
241	
242	// renderLearn formats a learn-mode result (teach or correct) for the result pane.
243	func (m Model) renderLearn(res engine.TranslateResult) string {
244		l := res.Learn
245		var b strings.Builder
246		if l.Direction == "correct" {
247			corrected := strings.TrimSpace(l.Corrected)
248			if corrected == "" {
249				corrected = res.Translation
250			}
251			b.WriteString(m.st.trans.Render("✔ " + corrected))
252			if s := strings.TrimSpace(l.Translation); s != "" {
253				b.WriteString("\n" + m.st.dim.Render(s))
254			}
255			for _, is := range l.Issues {
256				frag := strings.TrimSpace(is.Span)
257				if f := strings.TrimSpace(is.Fix); f != "" {
258					frag = strings.TrimSpace(frag + " → " + f)
259				}
260				if frag != "" && frag != "→" {
261					b.WriteString("\n" + m.st.warn.Render("✎ "+frag))
262				}
263				if e := strings.TrimSpace(is.Explanation); e != "" {
264					b.WriteString("\n" + m.st.alt.Render("  "+e))
265				}
266			}
267		} else {
268			tr := strings.TrimSpace(l.Translation)
269			if tr == "" {
270				tr = res.Translation
271			}
272			b.WriteString(m.st.trans.Render(tr))
273			for _, v := range l.Vocab {
274				head := v.Term
275				if v.Pos != "" {
276					head += " (" + v.Pos + ")"
277				}
278				if v.Phonetic != "" {
279					head += " " + v.Phonetic
280				}
281				b.WriteString("\n" + m.st.notes.Render(head))
282				if s := strings.TrimSpace(v.Meaning); s != "" {
283					b.WriteString("  " + m.st.alt.Render(s))
284				}
285			}
286			for _, ex := range l.Examples {
287				b.WriteString("\n" + m.st.alt.Render("✎ "+ex.Foreign))
288				if s := strings.TrimSpace(ex.Native); s != "" {
289					b.WriteString("\n" + m.st.dim.Render("  ↳ "+s))
290				}
291			}
292		}
293		if s := strings.TrimSpace(l.Notes); s != "" {
294			b.WriteString("\n" + m.st.notes.Render("ⓘ "+s))
295		}
296		for _, w := range res.Warnings {
297			b.WriteString("\n" + m.st.warn.Render("⚠ "+w))
298		}
299		return b.String()
300	}
301	
302	// renderSuggestions renders the ranked "did you mean" list for a dictionary miss.
303	func (m Model) renderSuggestions(res engine.TranslateResult) string {
304		var b strings.Builder
305		b.WriteString(m.st.dim.Render("no exact match — did you mean:"))
306		for i, w := range res.Suggestions {
307			b.WriteString("\n" + m.st.dim.Render(fmt.Sprintf("%d.", i+1)) + " " + m.st.alt.Render(w))
308		}
309		b.WriteString("\n" + m.st.dim.Render("↵ choose"))
310		return b.String()
311	}
312
```

```text
1	// Package tui implements the interactive Bubble Tea (v2, charm.land/*) front-end.
2	// It imports the shared engine layer but the engine layer never imports it.
3	package tui
4	
5	import (
6		"context"
7		"time"
8	
9		"charm.land/bubbles/v2/key"
10		"charm.land/bubbles/v2/list"
11		"charm.land/bubbles/v2/spinner"
12		"charm.land/bubbles/v2/textarea"
13		"charm.land/bubbles/v2/viewport"
14		tea "charm.land/bubbletea/v2"
15	
16		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/engine"
17		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/store"
18		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/tts"
19	)
20	
21	// NamedEngine is one selectable engine in the TUI's ^e cycle.
22	type NamedEngine struct {
23		Name   string // "auto", "google", "dictionary", …
24		Engine engine.Engine
25		Mode   engine.Mode
26	}
27	
28	// Params configures a TUI session.
29	type Params struct {
30		Engines       []NamedEngine      // selectable engines; index 0 is the default
31		ModelSource   engine.ModelLister // fetches the model list for the model picker (may be nil)
32		ModelProvider string             // provider name the model override applies to
33		Store         store.Store        // nil when history is disabled
34		Source        string
35		Target        string
36		Pair          bool          // bidirectional pair mode
37		PairWith      string        // the "away" language (resolved code)
38		Learn         bool          // start with learning mode (^n) on
39		LearnEngine   engine.Engine // bare LLM engine for learn requests (nil => learn disabled)
40		Model         string        // display model id for the footer (initial)
41		Preset        string        // LLM prompt style
42		Instructions  string        // extra system-prompt guidance
43		Live          bool          // live-debounce default state
44		DebounceMs    int
45		Speaker       tts.Speaker // TTS backend for ^s (nil when disabled)
46		Foreign       string      // preferred "副"/foreign language to speak ("" => derive)
47	}
48	
49	type status int
50	
51	const (
52		statusIdle status = iota
53		statusTyping
54		statusTranslating
55		statusDone
56		statusError
57	)
58	
59	// focusPane selects which pane keyboard navigation drives. Global bindings
60	// (Enter, ^y, ^e, …) work regardless; only ordinary/scroll keys are routed.
61	type focusPane int
62	
63	const (
64		focusInput  focusPane = iota // typing + cursor navigation in the textarea (default)
65		focusOutput                  // keyboard scrolling of the result viewport
66	)
67	
68	// inputH is the fixed height (rows) of the input textarea box; shared by the
69	// layout and the click-to-focus hit test.
70	const inputH = 4
71	
72	// Model is the Bubble Tea model. All methods use value receivers and return the
73	// modified copy (the v2 convention) to avoid "state didn't stick" bugs.
74	type Model struct {
75		p    Params
76		base context.Context
77	
78		ta          textarea.Model
79		vp          viewport.Model
80		hist        list.Model
81		langList    list.Model
82		modelList   list.Model
83		suggestList list.Model
84		presetList  list.Model
85		sp          spinner.Model
86		keys        keyMap
87		st          styles
88	
89		width, height int
90		ready         bool
91		overlay       overlayKind
92		focus         focusPane // input (default) vs output; drives scroll/typing routing
93		resultH       int       // result viewport height (kept fixed to avoid layout jumps)
94	
95		source   string
96		target   string
97		pair     bool
98		pairWith string
99		learn    bool // learning mode (^n): structured teach/correct via p.LearnEngine
100		preset   string
101		live     bool
102		engIdx   int // index into p.Engines (the active engine)
103	
104		// model picker state (session-cached)
105		cachedModels  []string
106		modelOverride string
107		modelsLoading bool
108		modelsErr     error
109	
110		// curEngine/curModel reflect the engine that actually served the last
111		// result (may differ from the selected engine when the auto chain falls back).
112		curEngine string
113		curModel  string
114	
115		status status
116		result *engine.TranslateResult
117		err    error
118		flash  string // transient footer notice (e.g. "copied ✓")
119	
120		// speakCancel cancels in-flight TTS playback (^s); a new ^s or Clear/Quit
121		// cancels the previous one so playback never overlaps.
122		speakCancel context.CancelFunc
123	
124		// request lifecycle: one monotonic seq drives debounce-collapse, cancel, and
125		// stale-stream-drop. cancel/stream/streamBuf belong to the in-flight request.
126		// streamBuf is a plain string (NOT strings.Builder): the model is copied by
127		// value on every Update, and copying a used Builder panics.
128		seq         int
129		inflight    int
130		cancel      context.CancelFunc
131		stream      <-chan engine.Chunk
132		streamBuf   string
133		debounce    time.Duration
134		lastInput   string     // input text that produced the in-flight/last request
135		lastDoneKey cacheKey   // key of the last COMPLETED result (skip identical re-runs)
136		pendingKey  cacheKey   // cache key of the in-flight request
137		cache       cacheStore // session result cache (live cache-hit = instant, no API call)
138	
139		// Truncation auto-retry: a truncated stream (rare copilot-proxy drop) is
140		// re-fired once automatically, since a fresh request almost always completes.
141		// autoRetryKey is the pendingKey we've already retried once (so we retry at
142		// most once per input); retrying drives the "retrying" placeholder label.
143		autoRetryKey cacheKey
144		retrying     bool
145	}
146	
147	// active returns the currently selected engine.
148	func (m Model) active() NamedEngine {
149		if len(m.p.Engines) == 0 {
150			return NamedEngine{}
151		}
152		return m.p.Engines[m.engIdx%len(m.p.Engines)]
153	}
154	
155	// New builds a TUI model.
156	func New(ctx context.Context, p Params) Model {
157		ta := textarea.New()
158		ta.Placeholder = "Type text to translate…"
159		ta.ShowLineNumbers = false
160		ta.SetVirtualCursor(true)
161		// Enter triggers translation, so move newline insertion to Alt+Enter.
162		ta.KeyMap.InsertNewline = key.NewBinding(key.WithKeys("alt+enter"))
163		ta.Focus()
164	
165		debounce := time.Duration(p.DebounceMs) * time.Millisecond
166		if debounce <= 0 {
167			debounce = 400 * time.Millisecond
168		}
169	
170		hist := list.New(nil, list.NewDefaultDelegate(), 0, 0)
171		hist.Title = "History (↵ recall · esc close)"
172		hist.SetShowHelp(false)
173	
174		langList := list.New(langItems(), list.NewDefaultDelegate(), 0, 0)
175		langList.Title = "Target language (↵ select · esc close)"
176		langList.SetShowHelp(false)
177	
178		modelList := list.New(nil, list.NewDefaultDelegate(), 0, 0)
179		modelList.Title = "Model (↵ select · esc close)"
180		modelList.SetShowHelp(false)
181	
182		suggestList := list.New(nil, list.NewDefaultDelegate(), 0, 0)
183		suggestList.Title = "Did you mean? (↵ look up · esc close)"
184		suggestList.SetShowHelp(false)
185	
186		preset := p.Preset
187		if preset == "" {
188			preset = engine.PresetConcise
189		}
190		presetList := list.New(presetItems(preset), list.NewDefaultDelegate(), 0, 0)
191		presetList.Title = "Style (↵ select · esc close)"
192		presetList.SetShowHelp(false)
193	
194		sp := spinner.New()
195		sp.Spinner = spinner.MiniDot
196	
197		// Soft-wrap long paragraph lines to the viewport width; without this a single
198		// long (unwrapped) translation line is clipped and its tail is lost on screen.
199		vp := viewport.New()
200		vp.SoftWrap = true
201	
202		return Model{
203			p:           p,
204			base:        ctx,
205			ta:          ta,
206			vp:          vp,
207			hist:        hist,
208			langList:    langList,
209			modelList:   modelList,
210			suggestList: suggestList,
211			presetList:  presetList,
212			sp:          sp,
213			keys:        defaultKeys(),
214			st:          newStyles(),
215			source:      p.Source,
216			target:      p.Target,
217			pair:        p.Pair,
218			pairWith:    p.PairWith,
219			learn:       p.Learn && p.LearnEngine != nil,
220			preset:      preset,
221			live:        p.Live,
222			status:      statusIdle,
223			debounce:    debounce,
224			cache:       cacheStore{},
225		}
226	}
227	
228	// Pair returns the current source and target language codes (read by the caller
229	// after the program exits to persist the last pair).
230	func (m Model) Pair() (source, target string) { return m.source, m.target }
231	
232	// Init requests the initial window size and starts the cursor blink + spinner.
233	func (m Model) Init() tea.Cmd {
234		return tea.Batch(textarea.Blink, m.sp.Tick, tea.RequestWindowSize)
235	}
236
```

1	package tui

3	import (
4		"context"

7		"charm.land/bubbles/v2/key"
8		"charm.land/bubbles/v2/spinner"
9		tea "charm.land/bubbletea/v2"

12		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/debug"

18	
19	// Update handles messages. Value receiver returning the modified copy is the
20	// single convention used throughout (see Model doc).
21	func (m Model) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
22		switch msg := msg.(type) {
23		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
24			m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
25			m.relayout()
26			m.ready = true
27			return m, nil
28	
29		case tea.KeyPressMsg:
30			return m.handleKey(msg)
31	
32		case debounceMsg:
33			if msg.seq != m.seq {
34				return m, nil // a newer keystroke arrived; this tick is stale
35			}
36			return m.launch(false)
37	
38		case historyLoadedMsg:
39			m.hist.SetItems(toListItems(msg.items))
40			return m, nil
41	
42		case modelsLoadedMsg:
43			m.modelsLoading = false
44			m.modelsErr = msg.err
45			m.cachedModels = msg.models
46			m.modelList.SetItems(modelItems(msg.models, m.currentModelID()))
47			return m, nil
48	
49		case tea.MouseWheelMsg:
50			return m.handleMouseWheel(msg)
51	
52		case tea.MouseClickMsg:
53			return m.handleMouseClick(msg)
54	
55		case spinner.TickMsg:
56			var cmd tea.Cmd
57			m.sp, cmd = m.sp.Update(msg)
58			// Animate the placeholder inside the (fixed-height) viewport so the
59			// layout never jumps while waiting for the first token.
60			if m.status == statusTranslating && m.streamBuf == "" {
61				m.vp.SetContent(m.translatingPlaceholder())
62			}
63			return m, cmd
64	
65		case flashClearMsg:
66			m.flash = ""
67			return m, nil
68	
69		case speakDoneMsg:
70			m.stopSpeak() // release the cancel; playback has ended
71			if msg.err != nil && m.base.Err() == nil {
72				m.flash = "speak failed"
73				return m, flashCmd()
74			}
75			m.flash = ""
76			return m, nil
77	
78		case streamMsg:
79			return m.handleStream(msg)
80		}
81	
82		var cmd tea.Cmd
83		m.ta, cmd = m.ta.Update(msg)
84		return m, cmd
85	}
86	
87	// handleStream applies one engine Chunk, dropping anything from a superseded
88	// request. This seq guard is the correctness mechanism: cancelled results can
89	// never render even if a goroutine emits one late.
90	func (m Model) handleStream(msg streamMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
91		if msg.seq != m.inflight {
92			return m, nil
93		}
94		switch msg.chunk.Kind {
95		case engine.ChunkToken:
96			m.retrying = false // real tokens are flowing; drop the "retrying" label
97			m.streamBuf += msg.chunk.Text
98			m.vp.SetContent(m.st.trans.Render(m.streamBuf))
99			m.vp.GotoBottom()
100			return m, waitStream(m.stream, m.inflight) // re-subscribe for the next token
101	
102		case engine.ChunkDone:
103			m.inflight = 0
104			m.status = statusDone
105			m.err = nil
106			m.lastDoneKey = m.pendingKey // remember what was translated (skip re-runs)
107			if msg.chunk.Result != nil {
108				r := *msg.chunk.Result
109				// A truncated stream (rare copilot-proxy drop) is re-fired once
110				// automatically — a fresh request almost always completes. Only after a
111				// second truncation do we settle on the partial text + ⚠.
112				if r.Truncated && m.autoRetryKey != m.pendingKey {
113					m.autoRetryKey = m.pendingKey
114					nm, cmd := m.launch(true) // force a fresh fetch of the same input
115					mm := nm.(Model)
116					mm.retrying = true
117					mm.vp.SetContent(mm.translatingPlaceholder())
118					return mm, cmd
119				}
120				m.autoRetryKey = cacheKey{} // settled (complete, or gave up after the retry)
121				m.retrying = false
122				m.result = msg.chunk.Result
123				// A truncated result keeps its partial text on screen (with a ⚠), but
124				// must not be cached or persisted as if it were the real answer — so a
125				// manual retry (Enter) actually re-fetches instead of replaying it.
126				if !r.Truncated {
127					m.cache[m.pendingKey] = msg.chunk.Result // write-through (force overwrites)
128				}
129				if r.Engine != "" {
130					m.curEngine = r.Engine
131				}
132				if r.Model != "" {
133					m.curModel = r.Model
134				}
135				m.relayout() // engine/model segment width may have changed
136				m.vp.SetContent(m.renderResult(r))
137				m.vp.GotoTop() // show a completed result from its start, not scrolled
138				// Don't persist a truncated result, or a suggestions-only result
139				// (empty translation, no entry).
140				if !r.Truncated && (r.Dictionary != nil || r.Translation != "") {
141					return m, saveHistoryCmd(m.p.Store, m.recordFor(r))
142				}
143				return m, nil
144			}
145			return m, nil
146	
147		case engine.ChunkError:
148			m.inflight = 0
149			m.retrying = false
150			m.autoRetryKey = cacheKey{}
151			m.status = statusError
152			m.err = msg.chunk.Err
153			m.vp.SetContent(m.st.errText.Render("✗ " + msg.chunk.Err.Error()))
154			return m, nil
155		}
156		return m, nil
157	}
158	
159	// isTextInput reports whether a key press produced a printable, non-whitespace
160	// character (Key.Text is set only for printable characters). Space/tab are
161	// treated as navigation so they can page the viewport instead of typing.
162	func isTextInput(msg tea.KeyPressMsg) bool {
163		return msg.Text != "" && strings.TrimSpace(msg.Text) != ""
164	}
165	
166	func (m Model) handleKey(msg tea.KeyPressMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
167		// An open overlay (history / language / model picker) captures keys.
168		if m.overlay != overlayNone {
169			switch msg.String() {
170			case "ctrl+c":
171				m.cancelInflight()
172				return m, tea.Quit
173			case "esc":
174				m.overlay = overlayNone
175				return m, nil
176			case "enter":
177				return m.overlaySelect()
178			}
179			cmd := m.overlayListUpdate(msg)
180			return m, cmd
181		}
182	
183		switch {
184		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.Quit):
185			m.cancelInflight()
186			m.stopSpeak()
187			return m, tea.Quit
188	
189		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.History):
190			m.overlay = overlayHistory
191			return m, loadHistoryCmd(m.p.Store)
192	
193		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.PickLang):
194			m.overlay = overlayLang
195			return m, nil
196	
197		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.PickModel):
198			if !m.learn && m.active().Mode == engine.ModeDict {
199				return m, nil // no model concept in dictionary mode (learn always uses an LLM)
200			}
201			return m.openModelPicker()
202	
203		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.PickPreset):
204			if m.learn || m.active().Mode == engine.ModeDict {
205				return m, nil // presets are LLM translate styles; learn has its own prompt
206			}
207			m.presetList.SetItems(presetItems(m.preset))
208			m.overlay = overlayPreset
209			return m, nil
210	
211		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.ToggleLearn):
212			if m.p.LearnEngine == nil {
213				m.flash = "learn needs an LLM provider"
214				return m, flashCmd()
215			}
216			m.learn = !m.learn
217			if m.learn {
218				// Learn is bidirectional; ensure a distinct "away" (foreign) language.
219				m.pair = true
220				if m.pairWith == "" || strings.EqualFold(m.pairWith, m.target) {
221					if strings.HasPrefix(strings.ToLower(m.target), "en") {
222						m.pairWith = "zh-TW"
223					} else {
224						m.pairWith = "en"
225					}
226				}
227			}
228			m.relayout() // footer content changed
229			if m.live && strings.TrimSpace(m.ta.Value()) != "" {
230				return m.launch(true)
231			}
232			m.clearResult()
233			return m, nil
234	
235		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.TogglePair):
236			m.pair = !m.pair
237			// Pair mode is a no-op when "away" equals the target; pick a distinct away.
238			if m.pair && (m.pairWith == "" || strings.EqualFold(m.pairWith, m.target)) {
239				if strings.HasPrefix(strings.ToLower(m.target), "en") {
240					m.pairWith = "zh-TW"
241				} else {
242					m.pairWith = "en"
243				}
244			}
245			m.relayout() // footer pair segment changed
246			if m.live && strings.TrimSpace(m.ta.Value()) != "" {
247				return m.launch(false)
248			}
249			m.clearResult()
250			return m, nil
251	
252		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.Copy):
253			if txt := m.copyText(); txt != "" {
254				if err := clipboard.WriteAll(txt); err != nil {
255					m.flash = "copy failed"
256				} else {
257					m.flash = "copied ✓"
258				}
259				return m, flashCmd()
260			}
261			return m, nil
262	
263		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.Speak):
264			return m.speak()
265	
266		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.ToggleLive):
267			m.live = !m.live
268			return m, nil
269	
270		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.CycleEngine):
271			if m.learn {
272				m.flash = "learn mode (^n to exit)"
273				return m, flashCmd()
274			}
275			if n := len(m.p.Engines); n > 1 {
276				m.engIdx = (m.engIdx + 1) % n
277				m.curEngine, m.curModel = "", ""
278				m.relayout() // footer content (engine/mode) changed
279				// Only re-translate automatically in live mode; otherwise switch the
280				// engine, clear the now-stale result, and wait for Enter.
281				if m.live && strings.TrimSpace(m.ta.Value()) != "" {
282					return m.launch(true)
283				}
284				m.clearResult()
285			}
286			return m, nil
287	
288		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.Clear):
289			m.cancelInflight()
290			m.stopSpeak()
291			m.seq++
292			m.ta.Reset()
293			m.focus = focusInput
294			m.ta.Focus()
295			m.result = nil
296			m.err = nil
297			m.streamBuf = ""
298			m.status = statusIdle
299			m.vp.SetContent("")
300			return m, nil
301	
302		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.SwitchFocus):
303			// Toggle which pane the keyboard drives. Blurring the textarea also dims its
304			// border (View keys off ta.Focused()); the result box highlights via m.focus.
305			if m.focus == focusInput {
306				m.focus = focusOutput
307				m.ta.Blur()
308				return m, nil
309			}
310			m.focus = focusInput
311			return m, m.ta.Focus()
312	
313		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.Translate):
314			// After a dictionary miss the input still equals the missed word, so a
315			// second Enter means "help me choose" — open the ranked suggestions.
316			if m.result != nil && len(m.result.Suggestions) > 0 &&
317				strings.TrimSpace(m.ta.Value()) == m.lastInput {
318				m.suggestList.SetItems(suggestItems(m.result.Suggestions))
319				m.overlay = overlaySuggest
320				return m, nil
321			}
322			return m.launch(true)
323		}
324	
325		// Output pane focused: the keyboard scrolls the result viewport. A printable
326		// character (not whitespace) snaps focus back to the input and is typed there,
327		// so the user can just start typing without pressing Tab first.
328		if m.focus == focusOutput {
329			if isTextInput(msg) {
330				m.focus = focusInput
331				cmd := m.ta.Focus()
332				var tcmd tea.Cmd
333				m.ta, tcmd = m.ta.Update(msg)
334				return m, tea.Batch(cmd, tcmd)
335			}
336			var cmd tea.Cmd
337			m.vp, cmd = m.vp.Update(msg)
338			return m, cmd
339		}
340	
341		// Ordinary keystroke: update the textarea, then (re)arm the debounce.
342		var cmd tea.Cmd
343		m.ta, cmd = m.ta.Update(msg)
344	
345		if strings.TrimSpace(m.ta.Value()) == "" {
346			// Input cleared: cancel in-flight work and clear the result pane.
347			m.cancelInflight()
348			m.seq++
349			m.result = nil
350			m.streamBuf = ""
351			m.status = statusIdle
352			m.vp.SetContent("")
353			return m, cmd
354		}
355	
356		if m.live {
357			m.seq++            // invalidate any pending debounce tick
358			m.cancelInflight() // stop any in-flight request; its results become stale
359			m.status = statusTyping
360			return m, tea.Batch(cmd, armDebounce(m.seq, m.debounce))
361		}
362		return m, cmd
363	}
364	
365	// translatingPlaceholder renders the fixed-height "translating…" placeholder,
366	// upgraded to a "retrying" label while an auto-retry after a truncation is
367	// in flight so the user sees why a second request fired.
368	func (m Model) translatingPlaceholder() string {
369		label := "translating…"
370		if m.retrying {
371			label = "translating… · retrying (stream truncated)"
372		}
373		return m.sp.View() + " " + m.st.dim.Render(label)
374	}
375	
376	// launch starts a streaming translation for the current input, cancelling any
377	// in-flight request first. It is the single place a translation begins.
378	// When force is false (the live/debounce path) it skips re-translating text that
379	// was already translated, to avoid redundant LLM/API calls.
380	func (m Model) launch(force bool) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
381		text := strings.TrimSpace(m.ta.Value())
382		if text == "" {
383			m.status = statusIdle
384			return m, nil
385		}
386		key := m.cacheKeyFor(text)
387		if !force && key == m.lastDoneKey && m.status == statusDone {
388			return m, nil // already showing this exact result
389		}
390		// Cache hit on the live/debounce path: render instantly with NO API call.
391		// Enter (force) always re-fetches and overwrites.
392		if !force {
393			if cached, ok := m.cache[key]; ok {
394				m.cancelInflight()
395				m.status = statusDone
396				m.err = nil
397				m.result = cached
398				if cached.Engine != "" {
399					m.curEngine = cached.Engine
400				}
401				if cached.Model != "" {
402					m.curModel = cached.Model
403				}
404				m.streamBuf = ""
405				m.lastInput = text
406				m.lastDoneKey = key
407				m.vp.SetContent(m.renderResult(*cached))
408				return m, nil
409			}
410		}
411		m.seq++
412		if m.cancel != nil {
413			m.cancel()
414		}
415		ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(m.base)
416		m.cancel = cancel
417		m.inflight = m.seq
418		m.status = statusTranslating
419		m.streamBuf = ""
420		m.retrying = false // a fresh launch clears any stale "retrying" label
421		m.err = nil
422		m.result = nil
423		m.lastInput = text
424		m.pendingKey = key
425		m.vp.SetContent(m.translatingPlaceholder()) // fixed-height placeholder
426	
427		seq := m.seq
428		ne := m.active()
429		eng := ne.Engine
430		target := m.target
431		pairOn := m.pair && m.pairWith != "" && ne.Mode == engine.ModeTranslate
432		if pairOn {
433			target = lang.PairTarget(m.target, m.pairWith, text) // home-lang input → away, else home
434			debug.Logf("tui pair route: home=%s away=%s → target=%s", m.target, m.pairWith, target)
435		}
436		req := engine.Request{
437			Text:          text,
438			Source:        m.source,
439			Target:        target,
440			Mode:          ne.Mode,
441			Stream:        true, // ignored by non-streaming engines (google/dict)
442			Model:         m.modelOverride,
443			ModelProvider: m.p.ModelProvider,
444			Preset:        m.preset,
445			Extra:         m.p.Instructions,
446			Pair:          pairOn,
447			PairHome:      m.target,
448			PairAway:      m.pairWith,
449		}
450		// Learn mode overrides the selected engine with a bare LLM engine and asks for a
451		// structured, non-streaming tutor reply routed by pair direction.
452		if m.learn && m.p.LearnEngine != nil {
453			eng = m.p.LearnEngine
454			req.Learn = true
455			req.Mode = engine.ModeTranslate
456			req.Stream = false
457			req.Preset = ""
458			req.Pair = true
459			req.PairHome = m.target
460			req.PairAway = m.pairWith
461			req.Target = lang.PairTarget(m.target, m.pairWith, text) // for display/history; engine sets its own
462		}
463		debug.Logf("tui launch: engine=%s mode=%d target=%s preset=%s learn=%v", eng.Name(), req.Mode, req.Target, m.preset, m.learn)
464		ch, err := eng.Translate(ctx, req)
465		if err != nil {
466			e := err
467			return m, func() tea.Msg {
468				return streamMsg{seq: seq, chunk: engine.Chunk{Kind: engine.ChunkError, Err: e}}
469			}
470		}
471		m.stream = ch
472		return m, waitStream(ch, seq)
473	}
474	
475	// cancelInflight cancels the current request (if any) and marks it as no longer
476	// in flight so late chunks are dropped.
477	func (m *Model) cancelInflight() {
478		if m.cancel != nil {
479			m.cancel()
480			m.cancel = nil
481		}
482		m.inflight = 0
483	}
484	
485	// clearResult drops the shown result. Used when a setting (engine/lang/model/
486	// style/pair) changes without an immediate re-translate, so the pane never shows
487	// output that's stale relative to the (now different) footer.
488	func (m *Model) clearResult() {
489		m.cancelInflight()
490		m.result = nil
491		m.streamBuf = ""
492		m.err = nil
493		m.status = statusIdle
494		m.vp.SetContent("")
495	}
496	
497	// copyText returns the text to place on the clipboard for the current result.
498	func (m Model) copyText() string {
499		if m.result == nil {
500			return ""
501		}
502		return strings.TrimSpace(m.result.Translation)
503	}
504	
505	// speak plays the foreign side of the current lookup/translation. When the output
506	// pane is focused it speaks the result explicitly; otherwise it uses the
507	// foreign-language ("副") preference. Playback runs off the UI thread.
508	func (m Model) speak() (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
509		if m.p.Speaker == nil {
510			m.flash = "tts disabled"
511			return m, flashCmd()
512		}
513		forced := tts.SideAuto
514		if m.focus == focusOutput {
515			forced = tts.SideResult // explicit output focus → speak the result pane
516		}
517		resText, resLang := "", m.target
518		if m.result != nil {
519			resText = strings.TrimSpace(m.result.Translation)
520			if resText == "" && m.result.Dictionary != nil {
521				resText = m.result.Dictionary.Word
522			}
523			if m.result.Target != "" {
524				resLang = m.result.Target
525			}
526		}
527		ch, ok := tts.Select(tts.SelectInput{
528			SourceText: m.ta.Value(),
529			SourceLang: m.source,
530			ResultText: resText,
531			ResultLang: resLang,
532			Foreign:    m.foreignPref(),
533			Forced:     forced,
534		})
535		if !ok {
536			m.flash = "nothing to speak"
537			return m, flashCmd()
538		}
539		m.stopSpeak() // debounce: cancel any playback still in flight
540		ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(m.base)
541		m.speakCancel = cancel
542		m.flash = "speaking…"
543		return m, tea.Batch(flashCmd(), speakCmd(m.p.Speaker, ctx, ch))
544	}
545	
546	// foreignPref is the preferred foreign/副 language: the configured value, else
547	// the pair-mode away language ("" => Select derives it).
548	func (m Model) foreignPref() string {
549		if m.p.Foreign != "" {
550			return m.p.Foreign
551		}
552		if m.pair && m.pairWith != "" {
553			return m.pairWith
554		}
555		return ""
556	}
557	
558	// stopSpeak cancels any in-flight TTS playback.
559	func (m *Model) stopSpeak() {
560		if m.speakCancel != nil {
561			m.speakCancel()
562			m.speakCancel = nil
563		}
564	}
565	
566	// currentModelID is the model id currently in effect (for the ✓ marker).
567	func (m Model) currentModelID() string {
568		if m.modelOverride != "" {
569			return m.modelOverride
570		}
571		return m.curModel
572	}
573	
574	// openModelPicker opens the model overlay, fetching the model list once per session.
575	func (m Model) openModelPicker() (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
576		m.overlay = overlayModel
577		if m.cachedModels == nil && !m.modelsLoading && m.modelsErr == nil {
578			m.modelsLoading = true
579			return m, fetchModelsCmd(m.p.ModelSource)
580		}
581		return m, nil
582	}
583	
584	// overlayListUpdate forwards a message to the active overlay's list.
585	func (m *Model) overlayListUpdate(msg tea.Msg) tea.Cmd {
586		var cmd tea.Cmd
587		switch m.overlay {
588		case overlayHistory:
589			m.hist, cmd = m.hist.Update(msg)
590		case overlayLang:
591			m.langList, cmd = m.langList.Update(msg)
592		case overlayModel:
593			m.modelList, cmd = m.modelList.Update(msg)
594		case overlaySuggest:
595			m.suggestList, cmd = m.suggestList.Update(msg)
596		case overlayPreset:
597			m.presetList, cmd = m.presetList.Update(msg)
598		}
599		return cmd
600	}
601	
602	// overlaySelect applies the highlighted overlay item and closes the overlay.
603	func (m Model) overlaySelect() (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
604		switch m.overlay {
605		case overlayHistory:
606			// Recall is an explicit "show me this" action: always translate.
607			if it, ok := m.hist.SelectedItem().(histItem); ok {
608				m.ta.SetValue(it.rec.Input)
609				m.ta.MoveToEnd()
610				m.overlay = overlayNone
611				return m.launch(true)
612			}
613		case overlayLang:
614			if it, ok := m.langList.SelectedItem().(langItem); ok {
615				m.target = it.code
616				m.overlay = overlayNone
617				m.relayout() // pair segment width changed
618				if m.live && strings.TrimSpace(m.ta.Value()) != "" {
619					return m.launch(true)
620				}
621				m.clearResult()
622				return m, nil
623			}
624		case overlayModel:
625			if it, ok := m.modelList.SelectedItem().(modelItem); ok {
626				m.modelOverride = it.id
627				m.overlay = overlayNone
628				if m.live && strings.TrimSpace(m.ta.Value()) != "" {
629					return m.launch(true)
630				}
631				m.clearResult()
632				return m, nil
633			}
634		case overlaySuggest:
635			// Picking a suggestion updates the input and looks it up.
636			if it, ok := m.suggestList.SelectedItem().(suggestItem); ok {
637				m.ta.SetValue(it.word)
638				m.ta.MoveToEnd()
639				m.overlay = overlayNone
640				return m.launch(true)
641			}
642		case overlayPreset:
643			if it, ok := m.presetList.SelectedItem().(presetItem); ok {
644				m.preset = it.id
645				m.overlay = overlayNone
646				m.relayout() // style segment width changed
647				if m.live && strings.TrimSpace(m.ta.Value()) != "" {
648					return m.launch(false)
649				}
650				m.clearResult()
651				return m, nil
652			}
653		}
654		m.overlay = overlayNone
655		return m, nil
656	}
657	
658	// handleMouseWheel scrolls the active overlay list, or the result viewport.
659	func (m Model) handleMouseWheel(msg tea.MouseWheelMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
660		if m.overlay != overlayNone {
661			cmd := m.overlayListUpdate(msg)
662			return m, cmd
663		}
664		var cmd tea.Cmd
665		m.vp, cmd = m.vp.Update(msg)
666		return m, cmd
667	}
668	
669	// handleMouseClick routes clicks. In an overlay it forwards to the list; in
670	// normal mode a click focuses the pane it lands in (so the keyboard scrolls it),
671	// except a click on the footer row opens the language picker.
672	func (m Model) handleMouseClick(msg tea.MouseClickMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
673		if m.overlay != overlayNone {
674			cmd := m.overlayListUpdate(msg)
675			return m, cmd
676		}
677		if m.height > 0 && msg.Y == m.height-1 {
678			m.overlay = overlayLang
679			return m, nil
680		}
681		// The input box occupies the first inputH+2 rows (border + inputH + border);
682		// everything below it (down to the footer) is the result box.
683		if msg.Y < inputH+2 {
684			if m.focus != focusInput {
685				m.focus = focusInput
686				return m, m.ta.Focus()
687			}
688			return m, nil
689		}
690		if m.focus != focusOutput {
691			m.focus = focusOutput
692			m.ta.Blur()
693		}
694		return m, nil
695	}
696	
697	// recordFor builds a history record for a completed result.
698	func (m Model) recordFor(res engine.TranslateResult) store.Record {
699		src := m.source
700		if src == "auto" && res.DetectedSource != "" {
701			src = res.DetectedSource
702		}
703		target := m.target
704		if res.Target != "" {
705			target = res.Target // effective target (may differ in pair mode)
706		}
707		return store.Record{
708			SourceLang:   src,
709			TargetLang:   target,
710			Engine:       res.Engine,
711			Model:        res.Model,
712			Input:        m.lastInput,
713			Output:       res.Translation,
714			Alternatives: res.Alternatives,
715			Notes:        res.Notes,
716		}
717	}
718	
719	// relayout recomputes component sizes for the current window. The input/result
720	// boxes are rendered at .Width(m.width-2); lipgloss treats that as the TOTAL
721	// width, so the inner content area is that minus the box frame (border 2 +
722	// padding 2 = 4) = m.width-6. Wrapping content to anything wider makes each line
723	// overflow the box and get re-wrapped, orphaning a character per line.
724	func (m *Model) relayout() {
725		if m.width == 0 || m.height == 0 {
726			return
727		}
728		footerH := m.footerHeight() // wraps to width; may be >1 on narrow terminals
729		compW := m.width - 6
730		if compW < 1 {
731			compW = 1
732		}
733		resultH := m.height - footerH - (inputH + 2) - 2
734		if resultH < 3 {
735			resultH = 3
736		}
737		m.ta.SetWidth(compW)
738		m.ta.SetHeight(inputH)
739		m.vp.SetWidth(compW)
740		m.vp.SetHeight(resultH)
741		m.resultH = resultH
742		overlayH := m.height - 2 // overlays use a single-line footer
743		if overlayH < 1 {
744			overlayH = 1
745		}
746		m.hist.SetSize(m.width-2, overlayH)
747		m.langList.SetSize(m.width-2, overlayH)
748		m.modelList.SetSize(m.width-2, overlayH)
749		m.suggestList.SetSize(m.width-2, overlayH)
750		m.presetList.SetSize(m.width-2, overlayH)
751	}
752
```

180	}
181	
182	// Translate performs a translation, streaming tokens when req.Stream is set.
183	// It always returns a channel that closes after exactly one terminal chunk, and
184	// dispatches to the Anthropic Messages API for Claude models.
185	func (e *LLMEngine) Translate(ctx context.Context, req Request) (<-chan Chunk, error) {
186		// A bilingual doc request carries its content in Segments, not Text.
187		if strings.TrimSpace(req.Text) == "" && !(req.Bilingual && len(req.Segments) > 0) {
188			return nil, ErrEmptyInput
189		}
190		model := NormalizeModelID(e.cfg.Model)
191		// Apply a per-request model override only when it targets this provider, so a
192		// copilot model id never reaches an Ollama fallback (which would 404).
193		if req.Model != "" && (req.ModelProvider == "" || req.ModelProvider == e.cfg.Name) {
194			model = NormalizeModelID(req.Model)
195		}
196		if isAnthropicModel(model) {
197			return e.translateAnthropic(ctx, req, model)
198		}
199		return e.translateOpenAI(ctx, req, model)
200	}
201	
202	// ModelLister is implemented by engines that can enumerate their models.
203	type ModelLister interface {
204		Models(ctx context.Context) ([]string, error)
205	}
206	
207	type modelsResponse struct {
208		Data []struct {
209			ID                 string   `json:"id"`
210			SupportedEndpoints []string `json:"supported_endpoints"`
211		} `json:"data"`
212	}
213	
214	// Models fetches the provider's model ids, keeping only those usable through the
215	// transports this engine speaks: OpenAI /chat/completions, or (for claude-*) the
216	// Anthropic /v1/messages endpoint. Ids needing only /responses are dropped.
217	func (e *LLMEngine) Models(ctx context.Context) ([]string, error) {
218		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, 4*time.Second)
219		defer cancel()
220		req, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodGet, e.endpoint("/models"), nil)
221		if err != nil {
222			return nil, err
223		}
224		e.auth(req)
225		resp, err := e.http.Do(req)
226		if err != nil {
227			return nil, err
228		}
229		defer resp.Body.Close()
230		if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK {
231			return nil, e.httpError(resp)
232		}
233		var mr modelsResponse
234		if err := json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&mr); err != nil {
235			return nil, err
236		}
237		var out []string
238		for _, m := range mr.Data {
239			if usableModel(m.ID, m.SupportedEndpoints) {
240				out = append(out, m.ID)
241			}
242		}
243		sort.Strings(out)
244		return out, nil
245	}
246	
247	// usableModel reports whether a model id can be driven by this engine.
248	func usableModel(id string, endpoints []string) bool {
249		if isAnthropicModel(id) {
250			return true // routed via /v1/messages
251		}
252		for _, ep := range endpoints {
253			if ep == "/chat/completions" {
254				return true
255			}
256		}
257		// No endpoint metadata (e.g. Ollama) => assume chat-usable.
258		return len(endpoints) == 0
259	}
260	
261	// finalize builds the terminal result from the accumulated translation text.
262	func (e *LLMEngine) finalize(full, model string, req Request) *TranslateResult {
263		res := &TranslateResult{
264			Translation: strings.TrimSpace(full),
265			Target:      req.Target,
266			Engine:      e.cfg.Name,
267			Model:       model,
268		}
269		// The plain-text prompt returns only the translation, so fill the detected
270		// source offline when the caller asked to auto-detect.
271		if src := strings.TrimSpace(req.Source); src == "" || src == "auto" {
272			res.DetectedSource = lang.Detect(req.Text)
273		}
274		return res
275	}
276	
277	// promptFor picks the (system, user) prompt pair for a request: the structured
278	// tutor prompt for learn mode, else the plain translate prompt.
279	func promptFor(req Request) (system, user string) {
280		switch {
281		case req.Bilingual:
282			return buildBilingualPrompt(req)
283		case req.Learn:
284			return buildLearnPrompt(req)
285		default:
286			return buildTranslatePrompt(req)
287		}
288	}
289	
290	// finalizeResult builds the terminal result, parsing structured learn output when
291	// the request asked for it.
292	func (e *LLMEngine) finalizeResult(full, model string, req Request) *TranslateResult {
293		switch {
294		case req.Bilingual:
295			return e.finalizeBilingual(full, model, req)
296		case req.Learn:
297			return e.finalizeLearn(full, model, req)
298		default:
299			return e.finalize(full, model, req)
300		}
301	}
302	
303	// finalizeLearn parses the model's structured JSON reply into a LearnResult. It is
304	// defensive — it strips a markdown fence and slices to the outermost object before
305	// unmarshalling — and on any failure falls back to the raw text as the translation
306	// (with a warning), so a malformed reply degrades gracefully instead of erroring.
307	// res.Target is set to the FOREIGN (away) language because res.Translation always
308	// holds the foreign sentence (the corrected sentence, or the translation).
309	func (e *LLMEngine) finalizeLearn(full, model string, req Request) *TranslateResult {
```
</details></tool-use>

10	// Preset selects the LLM translate prompt style. Only ModeTranslate LLM engines
11	// honor it; google/dict ignore it.
12	const (
13		PresetConcise    = "concise"    // terse direct translation (default)
14		PresetContextual = "contextual" // short list of common-sense translations
15		PresetDictionary = "dictionary" // translation + 1-2 example sentences
16	)
17	
18	// translateSystemPromptConcise drives the primary (hot-path) translation. It asks
19	// for ONLY the translated text so the reply streams token-by-token identically
20	// across every backend and is latency-optimal.
21	const translateSystemPromptConcise = `You are a precise, professional translation engine.
22	Translate the user's text into the target language faithfully and idiomatically.
23	
24	Rules:
25	- Preserve the meaning, tone, and register (formal/informal, technical/casual) of the source.
26	- The user's text may contain typos, slang, or ungrammatical phrasing. Interpret their
27	  INTENDED meaning and translate that; do not translate a typo literally, and never refuse.
28	- Output ONLY the translated text. No quotes, no explanations, no language labels,
29	  no commentary before or after.
30	- ALWAYS translate into the target language. A single word, name, technical term, or
31	  loanword written in a DIFFERENT language must still be translated — never echo it back.
32	  Only when the text is ALREADY in the target language should you return it (lightly
33	  corrected if it was misspelled).`
34	
35	// translateSystemPromptContextual lists translations across common senses.
36	const translateSystemPromptContextual = `You are a precise, professional translation engine.
37	Translate the user's text into the target language, showing how the translation changes
38	across the most common senses or contexts of the source.
39	
40	Rules:
41	- Preserve meaning, tone, and register. The text may contain typos, slang, or ungrammatical
42	  phrasing — interpret the INTENDED meaning; never refuse and never translate a typo literally.
43	- Output a SHORT list of 2-4 lines, one line per distinct common sense/context.
44	- Format each line EXACTLY as: "N. <translation> — <short context label in the TARGET language>".
45	- Order lines from the most likely/common sense to the least.
46	- If the text has only one natural sense, output a single line — do not invent senses.
47	- Output ONLY the list. No preamble, no headings, no commentary before or after.`
48	
49	// translateSystemPromptDictionary gives a translation plus example sentences.
50	const translateSystemPromptDictionary = `You are a precise, professional translation engine
51	that also gives brief usage examples.
52	Translate the user's text into the target language, then illustrate it with example sentences.
53	
54	Rules:
55	- Preserve meaning, tone, and register. Interpret the INTENDED meaning of typos/slang; never refuse.
56	- The FIRST line is ONLY the translation of the user's text — no label, no quotes.
57	- Then a blank line, then 1-2 example sentences that use the translation naturally.
58	- Format each example on two lines:
59	    line 1: the example sentence in the TARGET language,
60	    line 2: its translation in the source language (the language of the user's input),
61	            prefixed with "  ↳ ".
62	- Keep examples short and idiomatic. Output ONLY the translation and the examples —
63	  no headings, no numbering of the first line, no commentary.`
64	
65	// systemPromptFor returns the system prompt for a preset (defaults to concise).
66	func systemPromptFor(preset string) string {
67		switch preset {
68		case PresetContextual:
69			return translateSystemPromptContextual
70		case PresetDictionary:
71			return translateSystemPromptDictionary
72		default:
73			return translateSystemPromptConcise
74		}
75	}
76	
77	// pairDirective is appended to the system prompt in bidirectional pair mode. It
78	// makes the model own language detection (more robust than a pre-computed target
79	// for short/ambiguous input) and forbids echoing the input — the exact failure
80	// this fixes ("test" → "test" instead of "測試").
81	const pairDirective = `Bidirectional mode: the text is written in ONE of %s or %s. Detect which of the two it is, and translate it into the OTHER one. ALWAYS translate — never return the text unchanged, even for a single word, a proper name, a technical term, or a loanword.`
82	
83	// translateUserPrompt frames the concrete request.
84	const translateUserPrompt = `Source language: %s
85	Target language: %s
86	
87	Text:
88	%s`
89	
90	// buildTranslatePrompt returns the (system, user) messages for a plain-text
91	// translation request. Language codes are expanded to readable names so the
92	// model handles regional variants (e.g. zh-TW → "chinese (traditional)").
93	func buildTranslatePrompt(req Request) (system, user string) {
94		src := strings.TrimSpace(req.Source)
95		if src == "" || src == "auto" {
96			src = "auto"
97		} else {
98			src = fmt.Sprintf("%s (%s)", lang.Name(src), src)
99		}
100		tgt := fmt.Sprintf("%s (%s)", lang.Name(req.Target), req.Target)
101		system = systemPromptFor(req.Preset)
102		// In pair mode, let the model detect the input language and route to the other
103		// side, and never echo — this is appended to (not a replacement of) the preset
104		// so the chosen output format (concise/contextual/dictionary) is preserved.
105		if req.Pair && req.PairHome != "" && req.PairAway != "" {
106			system += "\n\n" + fmt.Sprintf(pairDirective, lang.Name(req.PairHome), lang.Name(req.PairAway))
107		}
108		if extra := strings.TrimSpace(req.Extra); extra != "" {
109			system += "\n\nUser preferences (apply where relevant, e.g. domain terminology):\n" + extra
110		}
111		return system, fmt.Sprintf(translateUserPrompt, src, tgt, req.Text)
112	}
113	
114	// translateBilingualSystemPrompt drives context-aware whole-document ("doc mode")
115	// translation. The model sees the FULL document (prose + code as context) and
116	// returns a JSON object mapping each prose segment's number to its translation, so
117	// it can use cross-segment context — e.g. that a bare `rg` is the command being
118	// documented, not an abbreviation — and stay in one consistent target variant.
119	// %[1]s is the readable target language name (used twice).
120	const translateBilingualSystemPrompt = `You translate terminal / CLI documentation into %[1]s.
121	
122	You are given the whole document as a numbered list of SEGMENTS. Some are prose to
123	translate; others are command/code lines marked "[code — context only]" and are
124	shown ONLY so you understand the document — never translate or echo them.
125	
126	Rules:
127	- Use the WHOLE document as context. A bare token like "rg" or "ls" on its own is
128	  the COMMAND being documented, not an abbreviation — do not expand or gloss it.
129	- Keep command names, subcommands, flags, file paths, URLs, and code verbatim.
130	- Preserve meaning, tone, and register. Interpret the intended meaning of typos; never refuse.
131	- Translate EVERY prose segment into %[1]s only — one consistent variant, never mixed.
132	- Respond with ONE JSON object and NOTHING else: keys are the prose segment numbers
133	  (as strings), values are the translations. No markdown fence, no reasoning, no
134	  commentary before or after the JSON.`
135	
136	// bilingualUserPrompt frames the numbered document for a doc-mode request.
137	const bilingualUserPrompt = `Source language: %s
138	Target language: %s
139	
140	Document segments:
141	%s`
142	
143	// buildBilingualPrompt returns the (system, user) messages for a doc-mode bilingual
144	// request. Prose segments are numbered (1-based, matching TranslateResult.Bilingual
145	// keys); code segments are shown inline as context and are NOT numbered.
146	func buildBilingualPrompt(req Request) (system, user string) {
147		src := strings.TrimSpace(req.Source)
148		if src == "" || src == "auto" {
149			src = "auto"
150		} else {
151			src = fmt.Sprintf("%s (%s)", lang.Name(src), src)
152		}
153		tgt := fmt.Sprintf("%s (%s)", lang.Name(req.Target), req.Target)
154		system = fmt.Sprintf(translateBilingualSystemPrompt, tgt)
155		if extra := strings.TrimSpace(req.Extra); extra != "" {
156			system += "\n\nUser preferences (apply where relevant, e.g. domain terminology):\n" + extra
157		}
158	
159		var b strings.Builder
160		n := 0
161		for _, s := range req.Segments {
162			text := strings.TrimSpace(s.Text)
163			if s.Code {
164				b.WriteString("    [code — context only] " + text + "\n")
165				continue
166			}
167			n++
168			fmt.Fprintf(&b, "%d. %s\n", n, text)
169		}
170		return system, fmt.Sprintf(bilingualUserPrompt, src, tgt, b.String())
171	}
172	
173	// learnDirection reports the learn-mode direction, decided OFFLINE via the same
174	// script-aware router as pair mode (lang.PairTarget): native (home) input → "teach"
175	// (translate + glosses); foreign (away) input → "correct" (grammar-correct + explain).
176	// Trusting this over the model's own guess keeps the two output shapes deterministic.
177	func learnDirection(req Request) string {
178		if lang.PairTarget(req.PairHome, req.PairAway, req.Text) == req.PairAway {
179			return "teach"
180		}
181		return "correct"
182	}
183	
184	// learnTeachPrompt drives native→foreign tutoring. %[1]s = native (home) language,
185	// %[2]s = foreign (away) language being learned.
186	const learnTeachPrompt = `You are a warm, encouraging language tutor helping a native %[1]s speaker learn %[2]s.
187	The user writes in %[1]s. Translate their text into natural, idiomatic %[2]s, then teach it.
188	
189	Respond with ONE JSON object and NOTHING else — no markdown code fence, no prose before or after it.
190	Schema (fill every relevant field; omit a field only when it genuinely has no content):
191	{
192	  "direction": "teach",
193	  "original": "<the user's %[1]s input, verbatim>",
194	  "translation": "<the idiomatic %[2]s translation>",
195	  "vocab": [
196	    {"term": "<key %[2]s word or phrase>", "pos": "<part of speech>", "phonetic": "<KK/IPA for English, pinyin for Chinese>", "meaning": "<concise meaning in %[1]s>"}
197	  ],
198	  "examples": [
199	    {"foreign": "<a short natural %[2]s sentence using the translation>", "native": "<its %[1]s translation>"}
200	  ],
201	  "notes": "<one short usage or register tip in %[1]s, optional>"
202	}
203	
204	Rules:
205	- Write every meaning, note, and explanation in %[1]s (the learner's language).
206	- vocab: only the key content words/phrases worth learning (at most 8); skip trivial function words.
207	- examples: 1-2, short and idiomatic.
208	- Interpret the intended meaning of typos/slang; never refuse. Output ONLY the JSON object.`
209	
210	// learnCorrectPrompt drives foreign→native correction. %[1]s = foreign (away)
211	// language being learned, %[2]s = native (home) language.
212	const learnCorrectPrompt = `You are a warm, encouraging language tutor helping a %[2]s speaker who is learning %[1]s.
213	The user writes a sentence in %[1]s that may contain mistakes. Correct it and explain, kindly.
214	
215	Respond with ONE JSON object and NOTHING else — no markdown code fence, no prose before or after it.
216	Schema (fill every relevant field; omit a field only when it genuinely has no content):
217	{
218	  "direction": "correct",
219	  "original": "<the user's %[1]s input, verbatim>",
220	  "corrected": "<the grammatically correct, idiomatic %[1]s sentence>",
221	  "translation": "<a %[2]s translation of the intended meaning>",
222	  "issues": [
223	    {"span": "<the problematic fragment of the input>", "fix": "<the corrected fragment>", "explanation": "<why, in %[2]s>"}
224	  ],
225	  "notes": "<one short encouraging tip in %[2]s, optional>"
226	}
227	
228	Rules:
229	- Write every explanation and note in %[2]s (the learner's native language).
230	- If the sentence is already correct, echo it in "corrected" and return "issues": [].
231	- List each distinct mistake as one issue; keep explanations concise and beginner-friendly.
232	- Interpret the intended meaning of typos/slang; never refuse. Output ONLY the JSON object.`
233	
234	// learnUserPrompt frames the concrete learn request. The system prompt already
235	// names both languages, so the user turn carries only the text.
236	const learnUserPrompt = `Text:
237	%s`
238	
239	// buildLearnPrompt returns the (system, user) messages for a learn-mode request,
240	// choosing the teach or correct prompt from the offline-detected direction. Codes
241	// are expanded to readable names so regional variants (zh-TW) read naturally.
242	func buildLearnPrompt(req Request) (system, user string) {
243		home := lang.Name(req.PairHome) // native
244		away := lang.Name(req.PairAway) // foreign
245		if learnDirection(req) == "teach" {
246			system = fmt.Sprintf(learnTeachPrompt, home, away)
247		} else {
248			system = fmt.Sprintf(learnCorrectPrompt, away, home)
249		}
250		if extra := strings.TrimSpace(req.Extra); extra != "" {
251			system += "\n\nUser preferences (apply where relevant, e.g. domain terminology):\n" + extra
252		}
253		return system, fmt.Sprintf(learnUserPrompt, req.Text)
254	}

```md
# Plan: Collapse excess blank lines in the TUI result pane

## Context

When translating multi-paragraph text, the result pane shows large vertical gaps
— several blank lines between paragraphs (see the user's screenshot: 2–3 blank
rows between each translated paragraph).

**Root cause (confirmed by tracing the code):** the excess blank lines live in the
translation *text*, not in styling or layout.
- The `concise` prompt tells the model "Output ONLY the translated text"
  (`internal/engine/prompt.go:21`), so the model faithfully reproduces the source's
  paragraph structure — and occasionally emits runs of 2+ blank lines.
- `finalize` does only `strings.TrimSpace(full)` (`internal/engine/llm.go:264`),
  which trims the ends but leaves *internal* blank-line runs verbatim.
- The `trans` style is just a color + bold, no padding/margins
  (`internal/tui/styles.go:41`), and the result viewport uses `SoftWrap`
  (`internal/tui/model.go:199–200`) which preserves blank lines exactly.

So nothing in the pipeline collapses the whitespace. The fix is a small
display-layer normalizer.

**Decision (confirmed with user):** apply in the result pane (streaming + final)
**and** in `^y` copy, so what you see equals what you copy. Leave engine output,
saved history, and TTS verbatim. Collapse runs of 2+ blank lines down to a single
blank line (paragraph breaks are kept; only the excess is removed).

## Changes

### 1. New helper — `internal/tui/text.go`
Add a small pure function (with doc comment matching the file's dense-comment style):

\```go
// collapseBlankLines trims trailing whitespace from each line and collapses any
// run of 2+ blank (whitespace-only) lines down to a single blank line, so a
// multi-paragraph translation keeps its paragraph breaks without the model's
// occasional extra vertical whitespace piling up in the result pane.
func collapseBlankLines(s string) string
\```
Implementation: split on `"\n"`, `strings.TrimRight(line, " \t")` each line, keep at
most one consecutive blank line, `strings.Join` back with `"\n"`. No regexp needed.
(Leading/trailing blank lines are already handled by the callers' `TrimSpace`, but
the helper is safe on them regardless.)

### 2. Apply at the three free-text render/copy sites
These are the only places the plain `Translation`/stream buffer becomes visible text
(the learn/dictionary/suggestions renderers build their own structure and are not
affected):

- **Streaming render** — `internal/tui/update.go:98`
  `m.vp.SetContent(m.st.trans.Render(collapseBlankLines(m.streamBuf)))`
- **Final render** — `internal/tui/view.go:199` (in `renderResult`)
  `b.WriteString(m.st.trans.Render(collapseBlankLines(res.Translation)))`
- **Copy** — `internal/tui/update.go:502` (in `copyText`)
  `return collapseBlankLines(strings.TrimSpace(m.result.Translation))`

Applying the same helper to both the streaming buffer and the final result means the
pane does not visually "jump" when the completed result replaces the live stream.

### 3. Test — `internal/tui/text_test.go`
Table-driven unit test for `collapseBlankLines` covering: 3+ blank lines → 1,
single blank line preserved, whitespace-only lines treated as blank, trailing
spaces stripped, no-blank text unchanged, empty string.

## Verification

- `go test ./internal/tui/...` — new helper test passes.
- `go build ./...` — compiles.
- Manual smoke (needs a configured provider): run the TUI, paste multi-paragraph
  text that has double/triple blank lines between paragraphs, and confirm the result
  pane now shows a single blank line between paragraphs; press `^y` and paste
  elsewhere to confirm the copied text matches what's shown.

## Out of scope
- Engine/history/TTS normalization (user chose display + copy only).
- Learn / dictionary / suggestions renderers (they own their spacing; no change).

```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-21 17:02:49Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```go
package tui

import "strings"

// collapseBlankLines trims trailing whitespace from each line and collapses any
// run of 2+ blank (whitespace-only) lines down to a single blank line, so a
// multi-paragraph translation keeps its paragraph breaks without the model's
// occasional extra vertical whitespace piling up in the result pane.
func collapseBlankLines(s string) string {
	lines := strings.Split(s, "\n")
	out := make([]string, 0, len(lines))
	prevBlank := false
	for _, ln := range lines {
		ln = strings.TrimRight(ln, " \t")
		if ln == "" {
			if prevBlank {
				continue // drop the extra consecutive blank line
			}
			prevBlank = true
		} else {
			prevBlank = false
		}
		out = append(out, ln)
	}
	return strings.Join(out, "\n")
}

```

```go
package tui

import "testing"

func TestCollapseBlankLines(t *testing.T) {
	tests := []struct {
		name string
		in   string
		want string
	}{
		{
			name: "collapses a run of blank lines to one",
			in:   "para one\n\n\n\npara two",
			want: "para one\n\npara two",
		},
		{
			name: "keeps a single blank line",
			in:   "para one\n\npara two",
			want: "para one\n\npara two",
		},
		{
			name: "treats whitespace-only lines as blank",
			in:   "para one\n\n   \n\t\npara two",
			want: "para one\n\npara two",
		},
		{
			name: "strips trailing whitespace on content lines",
			in:   "hello   \nworld\t",
			want: "hello\nworld",
		},
		{
			name: "no blank lines is unchanged",
			in:   "line one\nline two\nline three",
			want: "line one\nline two\nline three",
		},
		{
			name: "empty string",
			in:   "",
			want: "",
		},
		{
			name: "adjacent paragraphs across many gaps",
			in:   "a\n\n\nb\n\n\n\n\nc",
			want: "a\n\nb\n\nc",
		},
	}
	for _, tt := range tests {
		t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
			if got := collapseBlankLines(tt.in); got != tt.want {
				t.Errorf("collapseBlankLines(%q) = %q, want %q", tt.in, got, tt.want)
			}
		})
	}
}

```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-21 17:04:09Z)**_

Done. The fix is implemented and verified.

## What was wrong

The excess blank lines were in the translation **text**, not the styling. The `concise` prompt tells the model "Output ONLY the translated text," so it faithfully reproduces the source's paragraph structure — and occasionally emits runs of 2+ blank lines. `finalize` only does `strings.TrimSpace` (trims the ends), the `trans` style has no padding, and the viewport's `SoftWrap` preserves blank lines exactly — so nothing collapsed the excess.

## What changed

A small display-layer normalizer, `collapseBlankLines` (`internal/tui/text.go`): strips trailing whitespace per line and collapses any run of 2+ blank/whitespace-only lines to a single blank line — so paragraph breaks stay, but the pile-up goes.

Applied at the three free-text sites (per your **display + copy** choice):
- **Streaming render** — `update.go:98`
- **Final render** — `view.go:199`
- **`^y` copy** — `update.go:502` (so what you see equals what you copy)

Engine output, saved history, and TTS stay verbatim; the learn/dictionary renderers are untouched (they own their spacing).

## Verification
- `go build ./...` ✓ · `go vet ./internal/tui/...` ✓ · full `go test ./...` ✓
- New table-driven test `internal/tui/text_test.go` covers: 3+ blanks → 1, single blank preserved, whitespace-only lines treated as blank, trailing-space stripping, no-blank text unchanged, empty string, and multi-gap paragraphs.

To confirm live: run the TUI, paste that multi-paragraph text again — paragraphs should now be separated by a single blank line, and `^y` should copy the same.

One note: this collapses *display* whitespace but the model still emits it. If you'd later want the CLI one-shot and stored history cleaned too, that's the one-line "engine `finalize`" extension we scoped out — say the word and I'll add it.

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```go
package tui

import (
	"fmt"
	"strings"
	"testing"

	"charm.land/bubbles/v2/viewport"
)

// Diagnostic: does the viewport (SoftWrap) preserve a single blank line between
// CJK paragraphs, or multiply it? Run: go test ./internal/tui -run Diag -v
func TestDiagViewportBlankLines(t *testing.T) {
	raw := "第一段落，這是一個比較長的句子用來測試軟換行的行為表現如何。\n\n第二段落，同樣是一個需要換行的長句子看看中間會不會多出空行。\n\n第三段落。"
	collapsed := collapseBlankLines(raw)

	vp := viewport.New()
	vp.SoftWrap = true
	vp.SetWidth(40)
	vp.SetHeight(20)
	vp.SetContent(collapsed)
	out := vp.View()

	// Trim the trailing fill blanks the viewport adds to reach its height, then
	// report the blank-line runs inside the actual content.
	lines := strings.Split(out, "\n")
	// find last non-blank
	last := -1
	for i, ln := range lines {
		if strings.TrimSpace(ln) != "" {
			last = i
		}
	}
	content := lines[:last+1]
	fmt.Printf("=== raw blank runs: %v\n", blankRuns(strings.Split(raw, "\n")))
	fmt.Printf("=== collapsed blank runs: %v\n", blankRuns(strings.Split(collapsed, "\n")))
	fmt.Printf("=== viewport-rendered blank runs: %v (content rows=%d)\n", blankRuns(content), len(content))
	for i, ln := range content {
		fmt.Printf("%2d|%s|\n", i, ln)
	}
}

func blankRuns(lines []string) []int {
	var runs []int
	run := 0
	for _, ln := range lines {
		if strings.TrimSpace(ln) == "" {
			run++
		} else if run > 0 {
			runs = append(runs, run)
			run = 0
		}
	}
	if run > 0 {
		runs = append(runs, run)
	}
	return runs
}

```

```go
package tui

import (
	"strings"
	"testing"

	"charm.land/bubbles/v2/viewport"
)

// Replicates renderResult's EXACT path: collapseBlankLines -> trans.Render ->
// viewport, to prove the whole TUI display pipeline yields single blank lines.
func TestDiagFullRenderPath(t *testing.T) {
	// Mirrors the real model output for a multi-blank scrape: runs of [3,4,1].
	raw := "第一段。\n\n\n\n第二段。\n\n\n\n\n第三段。"

	st := newStyles()
	rendered := st.trans.Render(collapseBlankLines(raw)) // exactly what view.go:199 does

	vp := viewport.New()
	vp.SoftWrap = true
	vp.SetWidth(40)
	vp.SetHeight(20)
	vp.SetContent(rendered)
	out := vp.View()

	lines := strings.Split(out, "\n")
	last := -1
	for i, ln := range lines {
		if strings.TrimSpace(ln) != "" {
			last = i
		}
	}
	got := blankRuns(lines[:last+1])
	t.Logf("raw runs=%v  ->  full-pipeline rendered runs=%v", blankRuns(strings.Split(raw, "\n")), got)
	for _, r := range got {
		if r > 1 {
			t.Fatalf("blank run of %d survived the render pipeline; want max 1 (all runs=%v)", r, got)
		}
	}
}

func blankRuns(lines []string) []int {
	var runs []int
	run := 0
	for _, ln := range lines {
		if strings.TrimSpace(ln) == "" {
			run++
		} else if run > 0 {
			runs = append(runs, run)
			run = 0
		}
	}
	if run > 0 {
		runs = append(runs, run)
	}
	return runs
}

```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-22 01:09:19Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```go
package tui

import (
	"regexp"
	"strings"
	"testing"

	"charm.land/bubbles/v2/viewport"
)

var ansiRe = regexp.MustCompile(`\x1b\[[0-9;]*m`)

// renderThroughViewport mimics the real display path: style each line with trans,
// push through the SoftWrap viewport, then strip ANSI so blank lines are visible
// to the counter (styled blanks carry color escapes but no glyphs).
func renderThroughViewport(content string) []int {
	st := newStyles()
	vp := viewport.New()
	vp.SoftWrap = true
	vp.SetWidth(40)
	vp.SetHeight(30)
	vp.SetContent(st.trans.Render(content))
	lines := strings.Split(ansiRe.ReplaceAllString(vp.View(), ""), "\n")
	last := -1
	for i, ln := range lines {
		if strings.TrimSpace(ln) != "" {
			last = i
		}
	}
	return blankRuns(lines[:last+1])
}

// Proves the fix end-to-end: the SAME model output rendered WITHOUT collapse keeps
// its multi-blank runs, and WITH collapse (the shipped path) drops to max 1.
func TestDiagFullRenderPath(t *testing.T) {
	raw := "第一段。\n\n\n\n第二段。\n\n\n\n\n第三段。" // model output for a scrape: runs [3,4]

	without := renderThroughViewport(raw)
	with := renderThroughViewport(collapseBlankLines(raw))
	t.Logf("rendered WITHOUT collapse: %v   WITH collapse (shipped): %v", without, with)

	// Guard: without the fix the multi-blank runs must be visible (else the test is
	// blind and proves nothing).
	sawMulti := false
	for _, r := range without {
		if r > 1 {
			sawMulti = true
		}
	}
	if !sawMulti {
		t.Fatalf("counter is blind: expected multi-blank runs without collapse, got %v", without)
	}
	for _, r := range with {
		if r > 1 {
			t.Fatalf("blank run of %d survived the shipped render path; want max 1 (runs=%v)", r, with)
		}
	}
}

func blankRuns(lines []string) []int {
	var runs []int
	run := 0
	for _, ln := range lines {
		if strings.TrimSpace(ln) == "" {
			run++
		} else if run > 0 {
			runs = append(runs, run)
			run = 0
		}
	}
	if run > 0 {
		runs = append(runs, run)
	}
	return runs
}

```

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```go
package tui

import (
	"regexp"
	"strings"
	"testing"

	"charm.land/bubbles/v2/viewport"
)

var ansiRe = regexp.MustCompile(`\x1b\[[0-9;]*m`)

// renderThroughViewport mirrors the shipped result-pane path: style each line with
// trans, push through the SoftWrap viewport, strip ANSI (styled blank lines carry
// color escapes but no glyphs), and return the blank-line run lengths between
// content. Width/height are generous so short fixtures don't get truncated.
func renderThroughViewport(content string) []int {
	st := newStyles()
	vp := viewport.New()
	vp.SoftWrap = true
	vp.SetWidth(40)
	vp.SetHeight(30)
	vp.SetContent(st.trans.Render(content))
	lines := strings.Split(ansiRe.ReplaceAllString(vp.View(), ""), "\n")
	last := -1
	for i, ln := range lines {
		if strings.TrimSpace(ln) != "" {
			last = i
		}
	}
	return blankRuns(lines[:last+1])
}

// TestResultPaneCollapsesBlankLines guards the whole fix end-to-end: a model output
// carrying runs of blank lines (as when translating a terminal scrape) must reach
// the result pane with at most ONE blank line between paragraphs. The un-collapsed
// control also asserts the counter can actually see multi-blank runs, so a blind
// counter can't make this pass vacuously.
func TestResultPaneCollapsesBlankLines(t *testing.T) {
	raw := "第一段。\n\n\n\n第二段。\n\n\n\n\n第三段。" // runs [3,4]

	control := renderThroughViewport(raw)                     // no fix
	shipped := renderThroughViewport(collapseBlankLines(raw)) // fix applied

	sawMulti := false
	for _, r := range control {
		if r > 1 {
			sawMulti = true
		}
	}
	if !sawMulti {
		t.Fatalf("control is blind: expected a multi-blank run without collapse, got %v", control)
	}
	for _, r := range shipped {
		if r > 1 {
			t.Fatalf("blank run of %d reached the result pane; want max 1 (runs=%v)", r, shipped)
		}
	}
}

// blankRuns returns the lengths of each maximal run of blank (whitespace-only)
// lines that sit between content lines.
func blankRuns(lines []string) []int {
	var runs []int
	run := 0
	for _, ln := range lines {
		if strings.TrimSpace(ln) == "" {
			run++
		} else if run > 0 {
			runs = append(runs, run)
			run = 0
		}
	}
	if run > 0 {
		runs = append(runs, run)
	}
	return runs
}

```
